summary: Set 068 Session 3 of 6: Experiment B - the cadence study. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 high (S2 routed rec was sonnet/medium, but the S2 disposition flagged S3's statistical-honesty analysis as opus-warranted at the boundary, matching S1/S2; operator started this session on opus). BUILT the staged-snapshot harness on the S1 run_test cage and RAN Experiment B end-to-end, then wrote a cross-provider-verified cadence verdict against the FIXED S2 pre-registration. HARNESS (experiment-b/, all under docs/ - NO ai_router production change): build_snapshots.py emits 'numkit' as 5 MONOTONE snapshots (each file introduced once and frozen; later files genuinely import earlier ones); catalogue.json seeds 12 defects across the 4 pre-registered taxonomy classes (5 cadence-payoff, 2 coupling-blind, 2 always-visible, 3 no-coupling; class<->(d,vis) invariants + >=3/>=2-each minimums asserted PASS); grade.py grades PER-REPEAT (NOT union-over-K) with file-in-surface gating (an arm can only catch a defect whose own file is in its surface - enforces 'Q structurally misses cadence-payoff'), cost_model integration (pinned, unchanged), the band rule (resolved iff |mean_k|>band AND mean/median sign-agree), and the binding realized-early gate (c_k<=t0+1 in >=2/3 repeats); run_arms.py drives R(per-session routed)/Q(end-of-set routed)/E(end-of-set path-aware + the live run_test cage via a temp one-commit git repo of S5), raw-first (L-064-3), token-budget-no-verdict recorded as failed arm (L-067-1). PILOT (prereg Sec10) caught a REAL seed defect before spend: the two coupling-blind defects' own files leaked their cross-file contract in their docstrings (would have made them in-snippet, contradicting the coupling-blind label) - fixed the CODE to match the FIXED design (alias contract now only in registry.py; None-drop only in convert.py), re-ran affected cells, then confirmed GPT path-aware converges on the tiny trees (no L-067-1 budget blowup). SWEEP: K=3, two providers (gpt-5.4 + gemini-2.5-pro), 42 cells, no errors, all E arms converged with verdicts. RESULT: the cadence MECHANISM is real and large - R realizes the cadence-payoff early window 5/5 BOTH providers, and is earlier+cheaper than BOTH Q (g=4.0, s=66, resolved) and the path-aware replacement E (g=3.0, s=27-30, resolved) on the cadence-payoff class. BUT the pre-registered null controls are NOT clean: no-coupling shows a resolved R-vs-Q saving s=1.0 (a pure SURFACE-coverage artifact - BD12 is d=0 but Q-invisible, so Q pays the escape penalty; zero timing component) and always-visible shows a resolved R saving s=3.0 (pure 1-snapshot earliness). So clause A3 (clean controls) FAILS and clause B3 FIRES -> pre-registered verdict CADENCE DEFENSE DOES NOT HOLD, via clause B3 ('surface artifact / pure earliness, not Q-invisible cadence') - explicitly NOT B1's 'no cadence value' (B1 is false). Coverage@close: E gpt 12/12, E gemini 11/12 (misses the Critical coupling-blind BD6 - path-aware is NOT a perfect ceiling), R 10/12 (misses the 2 coupling-blind cross-file@intro), Q 4/12. S4 input: demote/retire-leaning, with the narrow R-vs-E cadence-payoff rework-timing edge as the countervailing keep argument. Symmetric mechanism audit (audit.json) verified every catch is a real mechanism identification (quotes), no removals. VERIFICATION: gpt-5.4 high R1 ISSUES_FOUND (1 Major: results.md overstated arm E - E gemini misses BD6/Critical not '1 minor', and E_google catches only 1 of 2 coupling-blind; 3 Minor: grade.py 'evidence file' wording, mean/median sign-agreement not folded into resolved, run_test live-use uncited) -> all fixed, echoes propagated across Sec 0/2/5/6 (L-065-1), grade.py sign-agreement folded in + re-graded (no cell flipped), run_test cited to raw/numkit/E_google_S5_k1.json -> R2 (all four resolved, 1 trivial new docstring nit) -> fixed -> R3 VERIFIED. Metered ~$1.41 total (experiment pilot+sweep ~$0.85; verification R1 $0.426 + R2 $0.127 + R3 $0.003). ai_router pytest suite UNCHANGED (no ai_router/ change this session; docs/ scripts not collected) - 1548 passed / 1 skipped. NO release (S6). NO production-code change. next-orch routed=claude-sonnet-4-6/medium (run S4 keep/demote/retire decision).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (route task_type=analysis) recommends claude-sonnet-4-6/medium for Session 4 ('Routed keep / demote / retire decision'): S4 ROUTES the keep/demote/retire decision through cross-provider consensus + operator confirmation and IMPLEMENTS a small, well-scoped router-config.yaml / workflow-doc / close-out change (which may be 'no code change - keep'). The hard analysis (the cadence verdict) is already done + verified; S4 consumes it, so it is a decision + small implementation, not a large coding session - capable work for sonnet-class at lower cost than opus, and it avoids the L-067-1 gpt-5.4-as-orchestrator budget risk while reserving the flagship models as consensus participants + cross-provider verifier. CAVEAT for the S4 operator: the Experiment B verdict is NUANCED (cadence mechanism real but confounded; 'does not hold' via the control clause with a genuine narrow rework-timing edge for routed) - the same statistical-honesty weighting that kept S1-S3 on opus may again warrant opus for the decision-weighing step; the operator confirms at the S4 boundary, and the decision itself must be routed to multiple providers (decision-time consensus).
